# Sarge LLM Documentation

This file bundles the core Sarge Markdown docs for coding agents and LLMs.

Canonical index: `/llms.txt`

---

# Install Sarge

Sarge is installed as a small hosted browser script. Each Sarge project has a project-specific pixel URL. Use the exact snippet from the project detail screen in the Sarge portal.

## Hosted Pixel Snippet

```html
<script>
  window._sarge = { queue: [["track", "page.view"]] };
</script>
<script async src="https://track.sargetrack.app/pixel.js?env={siteEnvironmentId}"></script>
```

Place the snippet in the document `<head>` or as early as possible in the page body. The queued `page.view` call is replayed after `pixel.js` loads.

## Track Events

After the script loads, call:

```js
window.sarge("track", "product.viewed", {
  product_id: "field-flask",
  product_name: "Field Flask",
  price: 42,
  currency: "USD"
});
```

Core event names:

```text
page.view
product.viewed
cart.added
checkout.started
purchase.completed
meta.pixel.fire
google.tag.fire
data_layer.push
```

Properties must be JSON-serializable.

## Test Traffic and User Impersonation

For test flows, open the target page console and run:

```js
impersonate('customer_123');
```

Future browser events in that project use `customer_123` as `userId` and include Sarge-owned test metadata:

```json
{
  "sarge_test": true,
  "sarge_test_mode": "impersonation",
  "sarge_tester_user_id": "project-scoped-tester-id",
  "sarge_impersonated_user_id": "customer_123"
}
```

Clear the override with:

```js
clear_impersonation();
```

This is debug labeling only. It does not authenticate as the target user or grant application permissions.

## Verify Installation

1. Open the target site in a browser.
2. Open the Sarge portal.
3. Go to Projects.
4. Open the project that owns the pixel.
5. Confirm events appear in the project event stream.

For agentic verification, create a temporary verification key in the Sarge project page and open `https://sargetrack.app/verify/{siteEnvironmentId}?key={temporaryVerificationKey}`. The `siteEnvironmentId` is the `env` query parameter in the pixel URL. The page shows recent events and auto-refreshes while the agent exercises the target app.

No public Sarge MCP server or npm skill package is currently published. Do not run `npx sarge@latest` or install `@sarge/mcp`.

---

# Sarge Event Reference

Use stable, lowercase, dot-separated event names. Properties should be JSON-serializable and should keep the same shape every time an event fires.

## Ecommerce Events

### `page.view`

```js
window.sarge("track", "page.view", {
  path: window.location.pathname,
  title: document.title
});
```

### `product.viewed`

Required: `product_id`

```js
window.sarge("track", "product.viewed", {
  product_id: "field-flask",
  product_name: "Field Flask",
  price: 42,
  currency: "USD"
});
```

### `cart.added`

Required: `product_id`, `price`

```js
window.sarge("track", "cart.added", {
  product_id: "field-flask",
  product_name: "Field Flask",
  price: 42,
  currency: "USD",
  cart_size: 1
});
```

### `checkout.started`

Required: `value`, `currency`

```js
window.sarge("track", "checkout.started", {
  value: 84,
  currency: "USD",
  cart_size: 2
});
```

### `purchase.completed`

Required: `order_id`, `value`, `currency`

```js
window.sarge("track", "purchase.completed", {
  order_id: "order_123",
  value: 84,
  currency: "USD",
  item_count: 2,
  products: ["field-flask", "map-wax"]
});
```

## Watchdog Events

Sarge emits watchdog events automatically when it observes common third-party marketing APIs after the pixel loads:

- `fbq(...)` -> `meta.pixel.fire`
- `gtag(...)` -> `google.tag.fire`
- `dataLayer.push(...)` -> `data_layer.push`

## Naming Rules

- Use lowercase dot-separated names.
- Do not rename events once dashboards depend on them.
- Keep property names stable across platforms.
- Prefer `snake_case` payload keys.

---

# Self-Hosting Sarge

Self-hosted Sarge is for teams that want the event API and database in their own cloud while keeping the same lightweight browser pixel and tracking model.

## Deployment Shape

```text
Website/App
  -> Sarge pixel
  -> Sarge API container
  -> Postgres
```

## Minimum Services

- Postgres database
- Sarge API service
- HTTPS endpoint for event ingestion
- Static hosting or CDN for the pixel bundle
- A Workspace and Site record for each tracked property

## Environment

```sh
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@host:5432/sarge?schema=public"
PORT="49828"
NODE_ENV="production"
```

## Test Event

```sh
curl -X POST "https://events.example.com/v2/events" \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "siteId": "site_123",
    "name": "TestEvent",
    "occurredAt": "2026-06-19T12:00:00.000Z",
    "sessionId": "manual_test_session",
    "userId": "manual_test_user",
    "properties": { "source": "curl" }
  }'
```
